title: Implement privacy mode
body:
There should be a configurable privacy flag preventing Visual Studio Code from making any outbound connections similar to macOS firewall stealth mode. This would be useful to prevent someone with access to github operations data or an administrator of github accounts from tracking you.
Visual Studio Code immediately on launch makes various outbound tcp connections over https to Microsoft Corporation
Some Visual Studio Code out-of-the-box behavior has been seen to contact github every 3 minutes
- VSCode Version: 1.16.0
- OS Version: Linux

body:
This is my Snippets file: ``` { // Place your global snippets here. Each snippet is defined under a snippet name and has a scope, prefix, body and // description. Add comma separated ids of the languages where the snippet is applicable in the scope field. If scope // is left empty or omitted, the snippet gets applied to all languages. The prefix is what is // used to trigger the snippet and the body will be expanded and inserted. Possible variables are: // $1, $2 for tab stops, $0 for the final cursor position, and ${1:label}, ${2:another} for placeholders. // Placeholders with the same ids are connected. // Example: // "Print to console": { // "scope": "javascript,typescript", // "prefix": "log", // "body": [ // "console.log('$1');", // "$2" // ], // "description": "Log output to console" // } "CDRL": { "scope": "html", "prefix": "cdrl", "body": [ "$3" ], "description": "Insert href for CDRLs" }, "FLDR":{ "scope": "html", "prefix": "fldr", "body": [ "

$1

", "\t
", "\t\t", "\t
", "
" ], "description": "Creates a new 'folder'" } } ``` 1 - create a new text file and chose html as the language. 2 - type cdrl & press [tab] -- Snippet is displayed 3 - press [tab] on 1st placeholder to select pdf. 4 - type 01 & press [tab] to move to 3rd placeholder 5 - type test1 and press [tab] then [enter] to go to new line. 6 - repeat steps 2 - 4. title: Can/Does VSCode handle system shutdown correctly?
body:
I'm not sure if this is a feature request, or a bug, or if its even doable, or if my underlying assumptions are correct. Apologies.
Today I had accidentally clicked on "restart" on my PC's start menu (I had meant to restart a machine I had RDP'd into).
Luckily I had notepad opened with some unsaved changes and it blocked the restart enough for me to cancel the restart process.
However VSCode was open the entire time and when I came back it was in a broken state: clicking on a reference in my C# code resulted in a message `actual command NOT FOUND`; there was a notification that the TypeScript service had shutdown after restarting 5 times and would not be restarted, etc.
My theory is that Windows tried to shutdown these helper processes individually but had not yet sent the VSCode main process the system shutdown message, and continuously sending shutdown to those helper processes that VSCode tries to start up in response to them being shutdown (or blocking their startup altogether).
I don't think Windows sends a "system is shutting down" message to processes in a deterministic order, so would it be difficult to find out what message triggered the shutdown? I guess that would only be possible if your helpers are all hosted under some VSCode process. If it is possible the feature request would be for the helper processes to be required to forward the "windows is shutting down message" to the VSCode main process, so that it can then not only stop trying to respawn processes but can also start shutting itself down properly.
What do you people think?

title: Improved visibility of high CPU extensions / language servers
body:
Lately while I'm using VSCode it's basically always 100% CPU. Pretty annoying. I have a few projects open but nothing extreme (I'm not editing the Typescript compiler for example). If I check in `top` the culprit is almost always `Code Helper (Renderer)`. Not very useful.
Using `htop` or similar you can see the full command, e.g. the one that is currently at 100% CPU for me is Pylance:
```
/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Frameworks/Code Helper (Renderer).app/Contents/MacOS/Code Helper (Renderer) /Users/timh/.vscode/extensions/ms-python.vscode-pylance-2021.3.1/dist/server.bundle.js --cancellationReceive=file:480af1fc50513db33a8cfd8bd43722210c2d8e8010 --node-ipc --clientProcessId=11234
```
However I have observer 100% CPU with basically all languages I use: Rust-analyzer, Pylance, cpptools and ESlint/Typescript. I'm guessing they're just buggy.
It's probably infeasible to fix all of those extensions, but it would be nice if VSCode at least worked around their bugginess. Currently the only effort it goes to here is the Process Explorer, but it usually doesn't even show these processes!
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/376842/111989381-9672bb00-8b09-11eb-8496-302bc3ccb351.png)
No mention of pylance there! In fact it's not even showing the right window - I have several open but it's showing some random window that isn't even focused. It should surely show all windows?
I suggest the following:
1. Fix the Process Explorer so it actually shows all windows & processes
2. Add a dedicated CPU usage view for language servers/extensions.
3. If a language server/extension is detected as using 100% CPU for more than a certain amount of time, show a notification with an offer to restart it.
4. If possible, add an option to throttle language servers/extensions (e.g. using `nice`).

I want to switch projects for an hour, and I don't want to be forced to dispose of every unsaved file buffer in my current workspace to do so. Hot Exit _does_ allow me to quit VS Code completely without disposing of unsaved buffers, but if I want to close a workspace without quitting the app, it forces me to dispose of unsaved buffers. title: A More Intuitive "Stage/Unstage Selected Range" UI Suggestion for Diff Editor
body:
VS Code 1.44.2 Source Control supports to stage a portion of changes in a file from popup menu as shown in this screenshot.

Please support this Source Control UI improvement. Thanks.

title: vscode shows an extra definition location
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.17.2
- OS Version: Ubuntu 16.04
I'm writing an LSP based extension for our CSS preprocessor.
When implementing onDefinition I see that while I send one definition in the response, vscode shows 2.
The second location is the line on which the defintion request was sent.
This makes it harder to find the real definition, and prevents Ctrl+click from going directly to the location I send.
Am I missing something? As you can see in the pic, I am only sending one location back (line 7).
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1898725/32329663-57632fd6-bfe6-11e7-8ae7-1ade597bdd5f.png)

title: gcc 11.1 on WSL Ubunto 20.04 does not give correct output
Regex find/replace: Cannot prefix newline
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.8.1
- OS Version: Windows 7
1. Open any file that is not empty and has multiple lines
2. Open the find-module
3. Enable regex-matching
4. Enter the last character from any line in your file, followed by \n. For example `a\n`.
5. Observe that no lines were found.
However, `.?\n` and `.*\n` works, but only highlights the newline character.
I have identified this error on my Windows 7 machine, but it does not reproduce on my Mac.

title: 'selectHighlights' doesn't select highlights
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.40.2
- OS Version: Ubuntu 16.04
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
This is the action bound to `ctrl-shift-l` by default.
There seems to be a mismatch between the action name (`editor.action.selectHighlights`) and its textual description (Select All Occurrences of Find Match). The behaviour seems to correspond to the latter.
For background, I am using a Haskell LSP plugin. When my cursor is on a variable or function name, all occurrences in the file are highlighted. This is the same information I get from the references view. But when I call `selectHighlights`, the editor seems to do a dumb text search - i.e. other entities, in other scopes, with the same name, are also selected. This is useless.
So is there actually any way to select all currently highlighted strings in the editor?

title: VS Code as syntax highlighter on a website like PrismJS but with VS Code themes and plugins
body:
I'm using PrismJS for a long time now and have never thought about using something else (besides from that there isn't a better lib than PrismJS) but for my portfolio I thought it would be time to use something newer/more optimized and then, while editing my portfolio in VS Code, I had that idea:
> "Why can I not use VS Code as my code highlighter on my website, with my preferred theme and plugins to copy the code to the clipboard or something like that? This would be soo cool and people could have a button >Open in VS Code< and the code will open a new file with the correct syntax etc. :o"
So that is the idea but I guess the implementation will be much harder than I think 😄. Besides that, I guess a PrismJS plugin to allow VS Code themes in PrismJS or "open in VS Code" would be awesome as well.
Wonder what you are thinking about that.

title: Code hinting/Intellisense for custom angularJS modules
body:
Would be really lovely if VSC can support hinting and intellisense for custom angular modules created by devs in a project and not just the default angularJS support.

2: return to a separate file say controller.js and create a new controller.
3: reference service from controller and as one is typing code hints and intellisense popup showing related function created in service.

title: VS Code Integrated Terminal Not Outputting Complete print() - PrettyTable()
body:
Version: 1.65.2 (user setup)
Commit: c722ca6c7eed3d7987c0d5c3df5c45f6b15e77d1
Date: 2022-03-10T14:33:55.248Z
Electron: 13.5.2
Chromium: 91.0.4472.164
Node.js: 14.16.0
V8: 9.1.269.39-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22000
I have been trying to use VS Code for some python coding for a class I am currently taking. We must use PrettyTable() to generate tables of data after having filled them with data. However, it seems that every time it comes to printing these tables, VS Code cuts off the ends of each table. In this specific assignment, one table should be 4,940,992 characters long, but it only prints the first 9,648 characters before stopping any output and the other table should be 648,516 characters long, but only prints 10,123 characters.
I noticed #38137 was very similar to this issue, however I am not able to print each line individually without headers printing with them. This has occurred with other scripts in the past, but I normally just use a different application like WingIDE to confirm all my outputs are accurate, but for this particular assignment WingIDE is incredibly slow and I would much prefer to use VS Code.
I should also mention I have been unable to find any patterns with the cutting of the output. It may be time based since I haven't found a link with character count.
Are there any other solutions to this weird output cap in VS Code?

title: Allow customization of the pop-up documentation box during auto-complete
body:
It is currently impossible to resize the documentation box or change its font size - it is not controllable by the upcoming `suggestFontSize` either. This leads to awkward situations where the documentation has a lot of line breaks and becomes unreadable:
![screenshot from 2017-06-24 19-59-15](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/918661/27511245-ba386530-5917-11e7-963c-18beffa420ad.png)
I propose the addition of a variable that controls the documentation's font size and one that allows to scale the boundaries of the documentation box by a scalar.

title: Search for sub-symbols via dot-notation
body:
I would like to request the dot-notation be supported when searching for sub-symbols in a file.
### Example:
```python
# file.py:

class C:
    x = 10
    y = 20
    

class D:
    z = 30
```
After we press `Ctrl+P` and type `@:C.` what should get returned is:
```
x
y
```

title: Make the quick open prefixes configurable
body:
on testing #2791:
I tried hard to use the dash ('–') for Working file QP but then realised that the trigger character is in fact a tilde ('~'). We should either use a font that has a better tilde character, or use a better character (who knows how to enter a tilde on a German keyboard?).
![2016-02-23 23-46-40](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1898161/13269519/692fbd32-da88-11e5-9d92-7e3c0d8a1ee1.png)
Since we are running out of usable characters, IMO we will have to find a better mechanism for QP.

title: Recently closed file not showing up in recents list
body:
Hi,
If I have a file that I've been editing and I close its tab, then I want to open it again, I should be able to go to the recents list and it should be right at the top.  But it is not in the main list.  If I click on More.. and search for it, it shows up.

title: Allow intellisense while filling in snippet tabstops
body:
Currently it seems that suggestions do not appear when filling in tabstops of a snippet. Is this an intentional design decision? Would love for this to work!
![example of suggestions not appearing](https://media.giphy.com/media/SXfKg2D4l51nte1wEo/giphy.gif)

title: Per-keystroke audio cue for multiple cursors
body:
I suggest/request the ability to turn on a per-keystroke audio cue when typing/deleting with multiple cursors active.
I see value both as an accessibility feature and as a bonus for people like me who occasionally lose track of the fact that multiple cursors are active--especially when all but one are off-screen.
I imagine it such that that only actual edits would generate the per-keystroke audio cue, and not cursor movement.

title: QuickOpen marks most recently used editor by default
body: The QuickOpen functionality is great since it first shows the currently open editors, and if we write a search string it continues to look for files which are not open as well. But would it be possible to make it mark the most recently used editor by default, so that calling quickOpen and then pressing enter directly takes us to the last used editor?

title: shortcut to close the search / edit views
body:

title: VSCode setup consultants, need professional help
body:
Hi everyone,
With every new release, dozens of new configuration options are added to VSCode, as well as new extensions being released on a regular basis. It is now really hard to even try to stay on top of it, without knowing if this or that feature could potentially lead to a productivity boost.
In larger repos/workspaces with complex configurations, it is becoming a productivity cost to not configure everything well, while leaving some space for individuals to customise VSCode to their own specific flavour. So I'm wondering if anyone has thought of, or is providing, consulting services to help teams get to the ideal configuration?
Getting 1-10% extra productivity from each developer because your IntelliSense always displays the right suggestions is a simple yet very rewarding idea, with real value, and we'd thereforce be willing to pay someone to help us get it right. For example, I guess that IntelliSense gives us proper recommendation 60-80% of the time as of now.
I'm sure there are other things we don't know, like hidden gems in the extensions marketplace that could help us too.
I know this is not the perfect place, but maybe other people have thoughts to share on this, and someone with the right experience could suddenly think about giving this a go :)

title: Enable specifying installation directories in npm.sh
body:
Hello,
Currently you are unable to specify installation paths for node modules used by Code via `scripts/npm.sh`, only the architecture. This is an issue for package management systems on Linux where specific packages may "own" a set of files or directories, and thus produces conflicts on the system when multiple packages attempt to own the same set of files.
I'm proposing the following options be added to `npm.sh`:
`--installdir=DIR`: This would enable you to specify a general installation directory:
- Beneficial for build systems that use isolated install directories (where the contents of it are then considered the contents of a binary package) away from the build directories.
`--node-modules=DIR`: This would enable you to specify **the root** of where you want node modules to be installed.
- Resolves potential file conflicts for packages and pollution of global node_modules.
Examples:
`--installdir=/var/ypkg-root/install/vscode/`: This would be the root of the file system. Installation of binaries such as those placed in `/usr/bin` would begin with the installdir, so it is conceivable that the directory would for the binaries would then be `/var/ypkg-root/install/vscode/usr/bin`.
- Note: This would only be on the isolated build system, the final contents installed on the user's system would still be /usr/bin, this would just specify where to install things on build-time.
- Defaults to `/`
- Sets something like $installdir (used below).
`--node-modules=$installdir/usr/share/vscode/`: This would install node modules to a `node_module` folder in `/usr/share/vscode`. This keeps it isolated from any `npm` or `nodejs` system packages and files. Atom does something similar, with installing things to (_for example_) `/usr/share/atom/resources/...`
Thoughts?

title: Regex does not support `\K`
body:
In a regex search, `\K`  will prevent the previously matched text from being in the overall match. See
> To overcome the limitations of lookbehind, Perl 5.10, PCRE 7.2, Ruby 2.0, and Boost 1.42 introduce a new feature that can be used instead of lookbehind for its most common purpose. \K keeps the text matched so far out of the overall regex match.
from https://www.regular-expressions.info/keep.html
It appears that `\K` is supported in pcre2, so shouldn't this work in vscode?  For example, if I wish to find every 9th line in a file, this regex should work:
`(?:.*\n){8}\K(.*\n)`  See [https://regex101.com/r/IjbZTP/1](url) with pcre option selected.  Then I could select all find matches or set cursors at all without the full match text being included in the find match - as it is if you use `(?:.*\n){8}(.*\n)` .  So I want to keep all that matched text out of the find match except line 9 for example.  But `\K` does not appear to be supported in vscode.
Version: 1.41.1 (user setup)
Commit: 26076a4de974ead31f97692a0d32f90d735645c0
Date: 2019-12-18T14:58:56.166Z
Electron: 6.1.5

title: Default to generic icon for extensions that do not have custom icons
body:
> This request comes from the Marketplace team.
Need to send additional json data as request body:
```
"assetTypes":["Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.Icons.Default"]
```
And add additional flag `0x02` to the current flags(this means include files). This would return the icon file as part of response if it exists.
For example current body for call from VS code will change to:
```
{"assetTypes":["Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.Icons.Default"], "filters":[{"criteria":[{"filterType":1,"value":"vscode"}]}],"flags":391}
```

title: OSX Install shell command description is not specific on quality
body: The command to install `code` in the $PATH on OSX always says `code` in the command palette but the message following says `code-insiders` or `code-alpha` if it is one of those builds. I was concerned about what it was going to do on the insiders and alpha builds, people may be wary of adding it if they're not sure whether it will install it as a new command.

title: Stuck in tabstop after deleting snippet text
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
- VS Code Version: 1.61.2
- OS Version: 10.15.7
1. In a custom snippets file add
```
	{
		"Snippet Test": {
			"prefix": "snippet_test",
			"body": ["test($1)"]
		}
	}
     ```
2.  In a new file, type `snippet_test` and select the snippet.
3. Backspace to delete the whole line. Or Delete Line command (Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + K).
4. Try typing
5. You are now stuck in the Tabstop until you hit Tab, Esc, click etc.
While I'm stuck in the tabstop I don't get any more autocompletion results. Example trying to get the snippet completion again after deleting the line:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6343165/138336079-7730e810-c1d5-4c2f-8e72-37fa6d38f0ac.mov
Note that you can replace the `$1` with `$0` in the snippet body to somewhat get around this issue.

title: File explorer that can be open in any window
body:
I was searching the issues and extensions and didn't succeed in finding something close.
Emacs has neat functionality that in normal window we can open a files explorer ([Dired](https://www.gnu.org/software/emacs/manual/html_node/emacs/Dired.html)) I find it very handy and more practical than explorer tree which can only be shown aside, which in turn takes valuable part of a screen most of the time
Short film showcasing how toggling between file explorer and typical file content view works in Emacs:
![2017-07-19 09 39 41](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122434/28356091-06f20162-6c67-11e7-9bc6-6b716254cf98.gif)
In VSCode it could just open typical file explorer with focus on file that were open in given window, then traversing the file explorer with keyboard and specified shortcuts should work, pressing enter on file should just open the file in same window.

title: Implement a search field in the References list
body:
It would be nice to have a **search field** in the references list so that we can search some strings (normal search or regex) only in the list of references. Basically, implement the search function VSCode already has into the list of references too, so that we can look only in the file holding references of a function, component, type, etc...
At the moment only **Refresh**. **Collapse All** and **Clear** are the buttons available in the list.
For example, after clicking "Find all references" on a React component, a list with 100 files holding a reference would open, and with this new feature, I'll be able to find specific words (for example props names) only among these references file.

title: The format of block comments in JavaScript is wrong, which makes it impossible to use d.ts intelligent prompt
body:
- 1.60.0
win10
Can I modify the template of comments? The current comment is / * XX * /, actually it should be / * * XX * /
Only the comment vscode of / * * XX * / can recognize and provide intelligent prompt
1. Open js file
2. Input a function
```js
function ddd() {}
```
3. Input comments and press ctrl+alt+/
```js
/* some commets */
function ddd() {}
```
4. Function ddd can't distinguish comments
5. However, the following comments are OK
```js
/** some commets */
function ddd() {}
```

Is there a flag or something we can change to bring back Git? I miss are being able to undo my commit.

title: Crash reporting: include crash details
body:
Would it be possible to add the actual details of the crash dump to the crash page so that we get an idea what kind of crashes are happening? I think it would be cool if we could:
* group crashes by crash details
* try to find similarities from the telemetry in terms of user interaction
I guess depending on the OS we would need different strategies to extract the actual error from the dump, but I think we do this already for the crash telemetry drashboard if I remember correctly.

title: Is there, else can we have, a command for quick opening symbols sorted by category, i.e quickopen w/ `@:` as base search input ?
body:
Vscode has a feature that lets you search all symbols in a file/workspace (`workbench.action.gotoSymbol`). it's super useful and I use it all the time. By default, it seems to sort results by name. Vscode exposes this command as `workbench.action.gotoSymbol` and it is bindable ofc.
Vscode also allows searching all symbols in a file/workspace and sorting the results by category: with QuickOpen and a base search input of `@:`. the only problem is I can't seem to find the corresponding command for it, or at least the one that is bindable to a key.
Would it be possible to add it ? (e.g. ` workbench.action.gotoSymbolByCategory` and `workbench.action.showAllSymbolsByCategory`)
Thank you.

title: Reset tab-state when file at original state
body:
Currently, when I open a file, do stuff and then undo it until its original state, the tab keeps its state to changed – and also ask me to save changes; although effectively none have been done which is quite confusing (eg. when just inserting a quick `console.log` and then undoing it).
It'd be nice if undoing would also reset the tab-state a file.

title: Trim the indents to the level of adjacent lines with
body:
For example, in this code:
```javascript
function fn () {
	
	const x = 1;
	
}
```
Setting `files.trimTrailingWhitespace` removes the tabs on the second and fourth lines.
It bothers me, and I wish it would work like this:
The second line should be left with a single tab, as well as the fourth line - the indentation on these lines should not be clear(reset to zero).
If the indentation on the second and fourth lines were double, it should be trim to one tab.
**Reason:** When the indent is clear, and I want to add something on an empty line, I have to manually add a tab to align the indent. It is cumbersome and inconvenient.

title: Support an option to disable-direct-write
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.1.1
- OS Version: Windows 10
I'm passing the --disable-direct-write command-line option to VSCode to work around the blurry font issue on Windows. This works fine, but every time VSCode updates, the installer overwrites my changes to the VSCode start menu shortcut and registry setting. It would be great if I could put the --disable-direct-write option somewhere in my VSCode settings that doesn't get overwritten when a new update is installed.

title: Improvements to Debug Console window
body: There is no command to scroll up and down the debug console. As a result, it is impossible to do this via keyboard shortcuts. Furthermore, while focussed on the line with the prompt `>` in the debug console, also provide a command to toggle between this prompt and the various other input line related to the Debug console: Filter, Clear Console, Maximize Panel Size and Close Panel.

title: MacOS Touch bar function keys control system functions (eg brightness)
body:
I have a macbook with touch bar and I've set up osx to always show the function keys in VSCode. So far this works but when I press a function key, eg F1, then instead of performing the VSCode action, it performs the underlying OSX action. For F1, it decreases brightness. F1 is bound to opening the command palette normally. When I hold FN, the touch bar changes to the system controls (eg brightness etc). The only way I can execute a real F1 command is by shortly pressing the FN key and pressing F1. Then it opens the command palette. title: Environment variables in %PATH% are not expanded
body:
> This issue seems to be a duplicate of #26048, but since that was closed and I had some more info I couldn't add, I created this one. ### Summary I have some environment variables set to some directories, and they're used in my %PATH% variable (System space, not user) and they're not getting expanded in the integrated terminal. If I type `echo %PATH%` I see the variables unexpanded, instead of the directories they point to (Here's the output) : ``` C:\Program Files (x86)\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\iCLS\;C:\Program Files\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\iCLS\;%SystemRoot%\system32;%SystemRoot%;%SystemRoot%\System32\Wbem;%SYSTEMROOT%\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;%SYSTEMROOT%\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\dotnet\;C:\Program Files (x86)\Bitvise SSH Client;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\Program Files (x86)\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\DAL;C:\Program Files\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\DAL;C:\Program Files (x86)\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\IPT;C:\Program Files\Intel\Intel(R) Management Engine Components\IPT;%PATH_PROGS%;C:\Program Files\PuTTY\;%ARINA_CLI_DIR%;A:/Prog/Dev/Rust/Win/CargoHome\bin;C:\Users\Dante\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\Dante\.dotnet\tools; ``` But variables in User space (Everything after `A:/Prog/Dev/Rust/Win/CargoHome\bin` above) are expanded, Here is their unexpanded version from system settings : ``` %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;%USERPROFILE%\.dotnet\tools; ``` ### Workaround Seeing this behavior I tried running VSCode as Administrator, and this time the variables are correctly expanded. ### Info VS Code version: Code 1.79.0 (b380da4ef1ee00e224a15c1d4d9793e27c2b6302, 2023-06-07T14:26:35.552Z) (Portable) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22621 Modes:

title: Add several basic predefined variables for use in launch.json - e.g.  ${uid}, ${gid}, ${user}, ${home}
body:
Please expose several basic predefined variables for use in `launch.json` like `${uid}`, `${gid}`, `${user}`, `${home}` (and potentially in `settings.json` too, as per https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/2809).
Example use cases:
1. Extremely useful for multi-user environments to avoid port or path conflicts. (See YAML below)
2. Generally similar use cases to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/2809 where one needs to specify paths to SDKs, GOPATHs and alike.
```json-with-comments
{
  "version": "0.2.0",
  "configurations": [
    {
      "type": "perl",
      "request": "launch",
      "name": "Perl-Debug",
      "console": "remote",
      "port": "${uid}",  // <---- HERE!
      "root": "${workspaceFolder}/.vscode/root",
      "program": "${workspaceFolder}/${relativeFile}",
      "stopOnEntry": false,
      "reloadModules": false,
      "sessions": "watch",
    }
  ]
}
```

title: Prefill reported issue body with version and OS when triggered from menu
body:
You can set the issue body using the query string https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/issues/new?body=[body] This would be convenient for the team to auto-fill this information when using Help > Report Issues, it would also help reduce an extra round trip if the community leaves out their OS and leave out the version. It will also give us the commit which will be increasingly useful information for insiders towards the end of the month. I can't figure out how to do new lines in the query string is the only issue, `\n`, `
` and `<br>` all seem to not work.

title: Dynamically change run buttons to debug when breakpoints are placed
body:
In a recent user study, we observed a participant struggling to discover how to run in debug mode. After they placed breakpoints in their testing code, they would hit the run button next to their test expecting it to start the debugger and hit the breakpoint. It would be nice if when a user adds a breakpoint, the run button (whether it's for run file or for run test) would start the debugger

title: Notebook implementations analysis and exploration
body:
There are now quite a few notebook like features contributed by extensions, which usually leverage Web View API and Monaco Editor. We may want to look into what we can do from the core side to improve the user and/or development experience for notebooks.
*--Update--*
To track the analysis of different existing notebook implementation, we put the samples and benchmarks in a dedicated repository [rebornix/notebook-test](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test), which consists of following content
* [x] Python notebook
* [x] [Samples](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test/tree/master/samples)
* [x] [Technical details](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test#technical-details)
* [x] Azure Data Studio notebook
* [x] Samples
* [x] [Technical details](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test#technical-details-1)
* [x] [Benchmark](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test/issues/1)

title: Implement blank operations
body:
Please consider implementation of the following features, shown here in notepad++.
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/4483325/25244389/219ad5bc-25c6-11e7-94d2-a55eda78f3ba.png)
the tab to space/space to tab feature is very convenient. In my case, i prefer tabs, but company style guidelines dictate 2 spaces. I can easily convert to tabs, edit a file then convert to spaces and commit changes. I like VS Code, but i would prefer to have this feature available to me in VS Code. Bonus points if you can make configurable keyboard short cuts.

title: Extensions: ability to detect light/dark theme
body:
It seems there's no way to detect the if the current theme is dark or light.
This can be a problem if the extension has complex css, like this one http://stackoverflow.com/questions/37257911/detect-light-dark-theme-programatically-in-visual-studio-code
It would be great if there was a way to determine the kind of theme applied from the extension

title: Is it possible to set a custom File icon theme to only a particular view?
body:
Hi.
I'm trying to develop a VSCode extension. Following the documentation, I was able to set custom file icons by modifying package.json. However, when I ran the extension it changed the file icons even for the default explorer view. I would like to have only the file icon specification affect only the custom view that I created. Is this possible?

title: fixing c++ bugs in javascript -
body:
Emscripten is a very good project to port c/c++ projects to javascript,
there is a feature called Manual print debugging, which prints out the line number of the given c++ code in the ported javascript code.
http://kripken.github.io/emscripten-site/docs/porting/Debugging.html
My feature request sounds crazy, but there is a valid reason.
Because of emscripten you can debug ported javascript code from c++,
what i would like to achieve, to make changes in javascript and automatically converted back to c++.
I think that functionality can empower the free source world.
Especially when you have a badly written aging project like ffmpeg and you would like to find bugs in it,
so you can use emscripten debug it in the browser and convert back the changes into c++.
So any freesource project which misses manpower can get free hands quickly as javascript is a well known and easy to use language for everyone.
There are special cases, but to check and validate an encoder algorithm make it more efficient programatically can be a valid reason to use that technic. If you would be able to read the algorithm in a better way and debug in a better way, you can get an idea to write far better algorithm than the heavily patented mpeg h264 and aac. (I think it's a common problem in every browsers nowadays)
Not to mention c++ projects, which has been ended because lots of resources was needed to fix bugs further. (Osmosis is a good example on open street map - everybody is using it as a google alternative, but the osmisis itself is in 0.55 version, and finished the development after 2016)

title: Wrap default `Enter` behavior in a command
body:
My situation is, I need to add more functionality to <kbd>Enter</kbd> key.
For example, using `|` to indicate the cursor, `==>` means pressing Enter
```
- item|       - item       - item
         ==>  - |     ==>  
                           |
```
I find it hard to implement with `onEnterRules`. So I override the default <kbd>Enter</kbd> behavior by adding a shortcut <kbd>Enter</kbd>.
But then the default indentation rules won't work any more unless I re-implement them...
As a comparison, I also override <kbd>Tab</kbd> and <kbd>Backspace</kbd> keys. The difference is, when I have no custom behavior to perform, I can just call `commands.executeCommand('tab');` and `commands.executeCommand('deleteLeft');`

title: [loc][Query][VSCODE] Localizability of string: When set to `false` IntelliSense never shows `customcolor` suggestions.
body:
Hi,
please help localization team to answer question from translator.
In case translator is not sure about context/meaning/localizability of string we raise a question to Core team.
Could you help please? Thanks!**
Source:
When set to `false` IntelliSense never shows `customcolor` suggestions.
Query:
Is "**customcolor**" supposed to be localized? Left in English for the time being. Please confirm. Please note that this also refers to other strings, such as
When set to `false` IntelliSense never shows `constructor` suggestions.

title: Double click tab to make it visible in the explorer
body:
The whole thing where VSC makes the selected file visible in the explorer tab is about 50% really cool and 50% completely horrible, because it so often does it when I really don't want it to. I ultimately had to disable it.
But, it's obviously still very nice to be able to get a file visible. I was thinking perhaps double click its tab to manually invoke that functionality instead of having it only work for the selected file. Of course double clicking the tab would make it the active file as well.

Starting vscode this morning, these keybindings were appended to the end of the "Default Keybindings". title: More complete Unity
body:
It's just OmniSharp and C# support, not Unity integration. Even with using a Unity Editor Plugin, nothing changes: We have only C# support without UnityEngine.UI, UnityEngine.EventSystems, UnityEngine.\* and UnityEditor namespaces!
Also we don't have any Boo or UnityScript support when we're using VS Code, So we had to switch back to the MonoDevelop.
What Unity Community wants from Visual Studio Code is:
1. Implementing UnityScript and Boo Support
2. Fixing assembly referencing problems (specially for UnityEngine.UI, but I think that it's a Unity problem, not an OmniSharp bug)
3. Intellisense for Boo and UnityScript (by using Roslyn, since they're .NET languages)

title: Prevent default "close editor" behavior of going to previously opened editor
body:
When closing an editor/tab in VSCode, it navigates back to the previous editor/tab you were using. Compare this behavior to Firefox or Chrome, which simply go to the next tab to the right.
This can be annoying, because it depends on state that is not visible. If possible, could you create a setting that depends on the horizontal locations of the tabs? This may be more predictable.

title: Polyglot Notebooks: keyboard shortcuts prevent using  HTML input fields in cell outputs
body:
I can not use input fields in HTML being displayed as a notebook cell output (red rectangles below).
For these input fields, notebook keyboard shortcuts are applied when I try to type a text.
As a result, many letters are not taken into account or even break the whole cell (M will change it to markdown, and X will cut off).
It looks like `!inputFocus` _when clause_ that should prevent such keyboard shortcuts is targeted only for cell input but not for cell output.
I think a new clause should be introduced that makes HTML input fields be usable in notebook cell outputs.

title: Sidebar items folding
body: The folding is very inconvenient for multiple panes/items . If a pane is pushed by other, it will keep the new size . The panes is mainly resized in long term, based on their purpose . So this behavior results in requirement to never get them pushed . That doesn't allow to use more panes than fit in the pane that is closer to the other end of the list *(i)* . And means that when any pane is temporally resized, it has to be resized back to avoid the 'sub-panes' size growing . Please make the panes: remember their size that've been set by resizing them with the up border/end, and try to restore it always when there is free space left in relation to the other unfolded panes' setting/preference sizes .

title: Missing Sublime Text command - expand selection to scope
body:
While building this [extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.sublime-keybindings) to map Sublime Text default keyboard shortcuts to VS Code, I found that this feature is missing.
The feature is split selection into lines.
**Default Sublime Text keybindings**
Windows / Linux: <kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>space</kbd>
Mac: <kbd>cmd</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>space</kbd>
![expand_selection_by_scope](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2449568/19566165/22e0b55c-969e-11e6-83ec-2ee96c932345.gif)
Today an [extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=letrieu.expand-region) exists that provides this feature. I believe we should bring this into the product.

1.  Open VSCode 1.13.1 Editor
2.  Enter a few lines of text
3.  Click to place the text cursor into one of the lines of text
4.  Use the Shift+Alt keyboard shortcut to initiate Multi-Cursor mode
5.  Click the up or down arrow to extend the multi-cursor to the next or previous line
OOPS, instead of working properly, a clone of the current line of text is inserted!
I tried Windows 7 and Windows 10.  I tried rebooting those.
Visual Studio 2015 and Visual Studio 2017 work fine in the same session side by side with VSCode not working correctly.
I was previously using VSCode multi-cursor editing as recently as yesterday until the 1.13.1 update.
How do I restore normal multi-cursor keyboard shortcuts to work like Visual Studio 2017 like before?
or What are the new shortcuts?
I'm posting this as an issue because it was unexpected loss of a very useful VSCode feature and it is now inconsistent with regards to the same feature in Visual Studio.

title: Notebook implementation in core (rendering / editor features)
body:
Follow up of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/88243, and the work is now happening in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/86632 . We will build a solid implementation of Notebook Editor in the core this iteration based on the [feature list](https://github.com/rebornix/notebook-test/blob/master/roadmap.ipynb) we curated.
We will focus on the rendering of inputs/outputs and basic cell manipulation commands first and then explore how to support editor features in native notebook editor. At the same time we should move off workarounds in the prototype to ensure the PR in a mergeable state.
- Rendering
- Outputs Mimetypes
- Metadata
- Cell Manipulation
- Editor features
- Engineering
- List View
- Webview
- CodeEditorWidget

title: Extension remains disabled after extension bisect
body:
- VS Code Version: 1.92.2
- OS Version: Manjaro Linux
To reproduce:
- make sure you have an extension that causes an issue (or make sure you have a bunch of extensions installed and just fake the following steps)
- Start an Extension bisect
- When the popup asks whether you can reproduce the issue, say Yes when it reproduces, and No when it doesn't
- you get to the end of the extension bisect and get an answer that says "bla bla bla has identified the extension Whatever as causing the issue  bla bla bla"
Expected: once I'm done with the extension bisect, all the extensions that were enabled before the extension bisect should be re-enabled. EVERYTHING should be as it was before the extension bisect started.
OR, you may prompt me and ASK ME to choose whether to return all extensions to their enabled/disabled status as they were before  the extension bisect, or leave disabled the one that has been identified as culprit to the issue.
Or AT THE VERY LEAST if you're going to unilaterally decide to disable the extension that was identified as causing the issue, then at least warn me of this.
Observed: the extension that was disabled during extension bisect remains disabled, WITH NO WARNING.

title: VSCode can't find binaries in my path (eslint)
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.8.0
- OS Version: Archlinux
Hi,
I have some binaries such as eslint, jshint or tslint in a user defined binary directory (`/home/user/.config/yarn/global/node_modules/.bin/`).
This directly is added to the `PATH` at session startup and is visible to vscode.
I checked in the `/proc/pid/environ` file of my running vscode (actually I checked the process that run the jshint server as well as the one for the workspace) that it has the PATH environment variable correctly set.
Still, when I open a `.js` file, I get the following message:
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/160975/21218484/1ff5b948-c2b1-11e6-8a80-b84be1f166c9.png)
It is the same for eslint:
```
[Info  - 10:21:51] ESLint server is running.
[Info  - 10:21:51] 
Failed to load the ESLint library for the document /home/vnoel/Linagora/Petals/dev/git/petals-cockpit-new/frontend/generate-headers.js

To use ESLint in this workspace please install eslint using 'npm install eslint' or globally using 'npm install -g eslint'.
You need to reopen the workspace after installing eslint.
```
Surprisingly I don't have the problem with tslint even though it is in the same path…

}

title: Multiple VSCode windows opened when opening a workspace or file directly.
body:
- **VSCode Version:** 1.17.2
- **OS Version:** Windows 10
There is a feature of vscode that caches unsaved documents after closing the editor.  When the editor is reopened the unsaved file is open within the editor giving the user a chance to save it.
This is a useful feature but its quite jarring when opening an instance of vscode via the _Open with Code_ commands as this forces two windows to be opened at the same time.  This may also occur when starting an instance of vscode via the command line + arguments.
I'd like to suggest only "resurrecting" the window with the unsaved documents/changes when opening code without a workspace or file.

title: Why does editor.lineHeight default to 0?
body:
`editor.lineHeight` has it's default set to 0 here https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/blob/master/src/vs/editor/common/config/defaultConfig.ts#L99
Should it actually be `GOLDEN_LINE_HEIGHT_RATIO` or `null` instead? https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/blob/master/src/vs/editor/common/config/defaultConfig.ts#L30
It's a little confusing seeing this, since 0 means the lines will be stacked on each other:
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2193314/15766804/85a9bee6-28f7-11e6-9291-84fc04c79469.png)

title: Allow to format on save based on file extensions
body:
The current `Editor > Formatting > Format on Save` allows you to automatically format the current file. The problem is that I want to avoid that behavior for specific file types (in this case YAML files) since the automatic format doesn't complain with my team style.
It would be great to be able to allow users to either configure how the formatting is applied or to disable it for certain file types.
Thank you for this awesome editor!

title: command line launcher accept line number
body:
When woking with command line tools (e.g. `git grep -n`) it is very convenient to be able to open file on a current line.
Same feature exists in vim: `$ vim some.file +155` will open `some.file` and move cursor to line 155.
Can we consider adding same functionality for vscode?
For example: `code some.file +155`

title: Opening already-open file from external link should reveal in already-open pane
body: If I have multiple editor panes open, and one of them has file A in it, but another pane happens to have the focus, and I click a link from another program (say, a terminal) that points to file A, then currently VSCode will open another view of file A in the pane that has focus. I would like, instead, for the pane that already has file A to get focus, and file A to be brought into view.

title: Make true absolute imports relative to source roots work
body:
I asked on SO here https://stackoverflow.com/questions/70888494/configure-true-absolute-imports-in-typescript
But just in case it's not possible, I'm requesting this feature.
Specifically:
```ts
import foo from '/foo';
```
should import `{sourceRoot}/foo`
where `sourceRoot` is `{pwd}/src`
Assuming it's not possible to do this via `tsconfig.json` already. If so, this is a request for docs.

title: Terminal Launching using npm script
body:
VScode should have a keyword launching multiple Integrated Terminals.
Something like:
`<VSCode Terminal Keyword> run script` will open another integrated terminal tab running the script.

title: Mark files as sensibles (like files with passwords)
body:
I'd love a feature where we can right click on a file and mark it as "Sensitive", so everytime we open it there is a confirmation button to click which unlock the contents.
It's pretty useful when we are streaming our screen or people are next to the computer, and we click inadvertently on a file containing credentials.

title: powershell automated function is supposed to be opt-in, not opt-out
body:
I see a mention to a new `terminal.integrated.persistentSessionReviveProcess` setting in the release notes of [version 1.61 - September 2021](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_61#_restore-terminal-sessions-across-application-restarts) (emphasis mine) :
> In this release, there is a new **opt-in** setting for recording and restoring the buffer and using it to recreate processes. Set `terminal.integrated.persistentSessionReviveProcess` to enable this.
I don't see a note stating that this option was turned to an opt-out setting.
Did I miss that part ?
extra note : where are those buffers saved on disk ? if the recorder records every output of every command, I would like to audit the buffers to check that they do not contain secrets (e.g : the output of `cat config.yml` run on a remote server ...)
_Originally posted by @LeGEC in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/136566#issuecomment-971461853_

For example, I use spell check extension and add some ignore words to it. If I do not open folder, then the ignore words list does not work.
Do I have any method to open VS Code with folder mode as a default way, by simply double click the file?

title: Need scrollTop, scrollBottom, and scrollToCursor commands for Home, End and Control-L key bindings on macOS
body:
Hi there,
If we follow Apple apps like xcode or Pages as an example for macOS text input key mappings, some things are fine in VS Code. ```cmd+up``` is mapped to ```cursorTop``` and ```cmd+down``` is mapped to ```cursorBottom``` by default which is great. We can map ```pageup``` to ```scrollPageUp``` and ```pagedown``` to ```scrollPageDown``` to follow macOS behaviour of not moving the cursor when these are pressed (PS should these be macOS defaults?).
But we cannot map ```home``` to ```scrollTop``` and ```end``` to ```scrollBottom``` because the commands ```scrollTop``` and ```scrollBottom``` do not exist! Therefore on my keyboard ```home``` and ```end``` do nothing in a text file.
Could these missing two commands please be added to allow standard macOS key mappings for ```home``` and ```end```?
BR,
Mark
PS: After scrolling one often uses ```Ctrl-L``` ([reference](https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT201236)) to return to the cursor position - would be great if suitable command for this standard macOS shortcut was also added, e.g. ```scrollToCursor```

title: Mouse not working at all in terminal + all terminal editor keybinding is occupied by vs code
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
- VS Code Version: 1.79.2
- OS Version: Windows 10.
1. Open Vs code
2. Open new terminal
3. SSH into a server,  for example: ssh username@192.168.2.87  (i was logged into an ubuntu server)
4. open some larger config file @ the server. for example:
sudo nano .bashrc
sudo micro .bashrc
### The first bug:
**the mouse does not work at all in does editors**. Nor in nano, nor in micro.
In different terminal softwares: "Micro editor" >> uses the mouse with no problem.
"Nano editor" could use the mouse with the  -m switch.
After two hours of ai chat and google and youtube i could not use the mouse in the terminal.
### The second bug:
If in nano or micro editor i try to do anything usual (save, exit, delete a line, copy/paste), there is a huge possibility that a vs code keyboard shortcut is conflicting with the nano and or micro editor.
- Ctrl+K in nano editor to delete a line >> no chance to use it in vs code terminal
- Ctrl+Shift+K in micro editor to delete a line >> does not work in vs code terminal
- ctrl+q to exit from micro editor in the terminal >> steps out from the terminal in vs code so you can not close the micro editor
vs code look promissing, but without the mouse usage in the terminal it has a "DOS" system productivity,
and with the keybindings conflict, using vs code to ssh is basically impossible
Bence Gyulai

title: Question on Build and Run From Source instructions: Node version
body:
The wiki [instructions](https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute#build-and-run-from-source) say that I'll need Node.JS, at least version 5.10.   For unrelated reasons, I'm reluctant to install anything later than the latest [LTS](https://github.com/nodejs/LTS#lts_schedule) release (v4.4.5) on the default PATH on my dev system.
Is v5.10 still the best release for working on vscode?   Any reason not use the 'Current' v6.2.2?
Is it possible to setup and use a side-by-side version of Node.js for the purpose of contributing to vscode on the same machine where latest LTS-compatible tools are built?     Any recommended approach would be helpful?
TIA

body:
I work with net core every day. It is possible to watch sources from github inside my visual studio code? Maybe you know about extensions which can decompile sources or redirect me to github page with source code.
This can be useful on other programming platforms, not only netcore.

title: Treat words with hyphens as one word in Autocomplete
body:

A picture is worth a thousand words so here's the difference between Notepad++ autocomplete including a word with a hyphen versus VS Code separating it before the hyphen.
I've already removed hyphen's from "editor.wordSeparators" but it does not change the behavior. Unsure whether it is a bug (not feature haha) of editor.wordSeparators or if that is not the intended sure of that setting but either way this would be a nice feature to have.

body:

- VS Code Version: Version: 1.88.0-insider (user setup)
Commit: 7107b38665e428a974c06b2c6e8f67a9a078aefd
Date: 2024-03-13T05:48:45.252Z
Electron: 28.2.6
ElectronBuildId: 27476517
Chromium: 120.0.6099.291
Node.js: 18.18.2
V8: 12.0.267.19-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631
1.   "editor.formatOnSaveMode": "modificationsIfAvailable",
"editor.formatOnSave": true,
2. With a document with lots of git changes.
3. And an extension that doesn't implement provideDocumentRangesFormattingEdits (only provideDocumentRangeFormattingEdits).
We're seeing our C++ extension can get sent hundreds (e.g. 500) of formatRange requests from VS Code which can take 10+ minutes to process. We don't implement provideDocumentRangesFormattingEdits -- can VS Code only send 1 formatRange request when an extension doesn't implement provideDocumentRangesFormattingEdits or do we need to implement that?

title: Unable to remove statically forwarded ports
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
- VS Code Version: 1.75.1
- OS Version: MacOS Ventura 13.2.1
1.  Run a service inside a devcontainer
2.  From the devcontainer try to run a server on port localhost:8181
3.  Check the ports tab
There seems to be some automatic port forwarding happening on my machine. I am unable to hit localhost:8181 on which the service is actually supposed to be running. Besides that, there seems to be no option to remove the statically forwarded ports.
I have set Remote : Auto Forward ports to off in the settings, so the auto port forwarding shouldn't ideally be happening. If it is, there should be an option to remove these.
![Untitled](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50285787/221849881-d09aa57d-d7e0-4971-9987-40f41192fde0.png)
Also, my devconatiner.json has "forwardPorts": [], I am not even sure where this port forwarding his happening from, but there should definitely be an option to edit this or remove them completely

title: Omnisharp proeblem in Ubuntu 20.04 desktop
body:
Omnisharp fail I solved in this way
Note about using .NET Core 3.1.40x SDKs
The .NET 3.1.40x SDKs require version 16.7 of MSBuild.
For MacOS and Linux users who have Mono installed, this means you will need to set **omnisharp.useGlobalMono to never** until a version of Mono ships with MSBuild 16.7

title: Open file at line and row number by html link
body:
It would be nice, if we can open vscode with a specific file and line/row number with a link.
Background:
We have a npm script which gets us all files with sepecific regex matches and put them in a html page and we want to open these files (with line/row numbers) from a link.
Is there a chance to get this feature?
We image something like this:
`<a href="vscode://C:/temp/file.txt:51:120">`

title: name quick fixes that should run on save
body:
testing #14299
Instead (or in addition) I wonder if it would be more useful to name the quick fixes that I want to run on save. This might not be a problem today as only a small subset of problems are auto-fixable but I can see conflicts from warnings that you want to be aware of vs warning you want to auto-magically fix (think of remove unused import).

title: Allow the ability to "preview" large files
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.8.1
- OS Version: Mac OSX Sierra
I often have to deal with large text files and currently if I want to just take a peek at one I have to drop to powershell and remember how `Get-Content` works so I can pull off the top 100 lines of the file then open it in VS Code. I have this problem in most editors due to the size of the files. Say 500MB+ CSV dumps.
I would love if VS Code could look at the file size and suggest a "peek" of the Top 100/500 lines etc.

title: Add API to support custom width of document/webview columns
body:
As of now, it is possible to assign view-columns to API created documents/webviews. But, it is not possible to set their width ( in pixels or otherwise ) . This is needed in some extensions that use webviews, and  only need a small amount of space beside the main editor.
Currently, the user manually needs to resize them. Ability to do this programmatically ( set pixel widths of documents ) would be a nice addition to the API.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7714968/72125759-c7951e80-338f-11ea-9c35-1a6ce6e2cc03.png)
For example, in this image, the webview on the side is taking up very little space, but this had to be done manually.

title: Cursor doesn't always follow line indentation
body:
If you start a new block of code, the cursor auto indents when you create a line break (new line) for the body of the block e.g
class Test {
Here the opening curly brace of the block is followed by two line breaks before the closing brace.
If you arrow up now, the cursor doesn't stay inline with the indentation, but jumps to the beginning of the previous line.
The behaviour is the same using this code style too
class Test
// Some comment huh
Looking at the settings page and searching for 'cursor', there is not an option to change this behaviour from what I can see.
I work with C# but presume this happens in other languages, and wanted to know if this is a bug or if I'm doing something wrong.
Possibly irrelevant, but I don't see this in other IDEs I use such as IntelliJ where the cursor always follows line indentation when moving between lines.

title: Document continuous test tasks setup with PowerShell Pester
body:
Hi !
This is one about documentation on how to setup VSCode with PowerShell and Pester running tests continuously with tasks
1. Develop awesome PowerShell scripts / modules / DSC
2. Have awesome tests
3. Wanting to have those tests run on file changes
4. searching for docs on vscode https://code.visualstudio.com/Search?q=pester
5. can't find documentation
6. resorting to google and solves the problem, but would be nice to see some PowerShell love as well as it's barely even mentioned on vscode site @jpsnover

title: Clicking on path in exception traceback shows error on Windows
body:
### Applies To
### What happened?
There are clickable file path links in notebook cells exception tracebacks error output. But clicking them on Windows shows an error instead of opening the file.

import pathlib

title: Add option to let new windows cascade when set to inherit
body:
- VSCode Version: 1.9.1
- OS Version: Windows 10
1. Open a new window from an existing window.
This is related to: New windows should get the size of the previous active window #10864. It's great that the new setting ("window.newWindowDimensions": "inherit") was added to preserve the window size.
But, the new window overlaps exactly on the existing window. It might be a little bit confusing. A user may think that the previous window was closed. Or, was replaced by a new one. I think it should follow the behavior of widely used applications such as Windows Explorer or Firefox, both of which cascade the new window a little bit so as to differentiate it with the previous one.
Probably a new configuration setting such as, "window.newWindowPosition" with options, "same" and "cascade" could be added.

title: FormatOnSave.exceptions settings property
body: When working with git submodules I would like to have the `formationOnSave` option enabled for certain submodules/folders while having it disabled for other submodules/folders. I can currently achieve this behaviour by creating separate vscode workspaces for each submodule and then open each workspace separately. While doing so I set the `"editor.formatOnSave:"` option in the main `settings.json` to false and I set `"editor.formatOnSave"` in the ".vscode/settings.json" to true or false. I, however, think to have an `editor.formatOnSave.exceptions` property or a FormatOnSave toggle in the status bar would be a very helpful addition to have when working with submodules. Another way would be to use the `editor.formatOnSave` property of the `~/.vscode/settings.json` file inside a submodule/folder if it is present.

title: How to Remove Configuration Data of Deleted Plugins in VSCode?
body:
While checking my VSCode configuration file (settings.json) today, I noticed it still contains configuration data from plugins I’ve already removed. For example, I once used the Codegeex plugin, but after uninstalling it, its configuration details remain in the settings.json file. I’d like to clean up this leftover data, but I’m worried about accidentally deleting essential settings if I do it manually one by one. Is there a way to automatically remove all configuration data tied to deleted plugins in one go?

title: shortcut name: might goToDefinition be better than goToDeclaration?
body:
Hello,
About the key shortcut
``` son
{ "key": "f12",                   "command": "editor.action.goToDeclaration",
                                     "when": "editorTextFocus" },
```
We know that `definition` and `declaratoin` are different in programming, and the actual action of f12 is going to definition, not declaration.
Maybe this is a subtle issue, but it will be good for a better vs code.

title: In the Find/Replace widget, labels for the "Replace" and "Replace All" buttons don't show keybindings
body:
In the Find widget, the "Previous match" and "Next match" buttons have labels which include their associated keybindings in parentheses. The "Replace" and "Replace All" buttons in the Replace Input box don't display keyboard shortcuts.
I looked into this a bit before creating an issue, and the solution isn't the simple "look up keybinding --> add to label" approach that I hoped for. It looks like Replace and Replace All don't actually have keybindings defined. Instead, the keys are hardcoded into the `_onReplaceInputKeyDown()` method of [findWidget.ts](https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/blob/85de39a54d78de5d247b57d1e2c50cc81192212e/src/vs/editor/contrib/find/browser/findWidget.ts#L284-L292).
I would like to see Replace and Replace All get configurable keyboard shortcuts. The defaults would come from the existing hardcoded values, so the core functionality would be unchanged. With configurable keyboard shortcuts it would be easier to display them cleanly on the Replace and Replace All buttons, and we get some flexibility as a free bonus.
I would like to implement this and submit a PR, but wanted to document and perhaps discuss the issue first to make sure that sort of contribution would be OK. Some comments in #406 brushed up against what I'm suggesting here, but I didn't find any other potential duplicate issues.

title: [Extension API] vscode.workspace.textDocuments does not return all documents at the beginning
body:
I am using `vscode.workspace.textDocuments` to detect all opened and visible text documents.
At the beginning, when I start debugging my extension, I have 3 open text documents, lets say `test_1.php`, `test_2.php`, `test_3.php`.
Now when I access `vscode.workspace.textDocuments`, I only get the active one (in that example `test_1.php`).
When I click on another one (`test_2.php`) and access `vscode.workspace.textDocuments` again, I get the new active document and the previous one (`test_1.php` and `test_2.php`).
When I click on the last, remaining one (`test_3.php`) and access `vscode.workspace.textDocuments` again, I get the list of all documents.
But it seems to depend on the situation, which document has been active at the beginning or has been activated after I have clicked on it.
Is this a bug or a feature?
If a feature: Is there another way to access an up-to-date list of all opened documents?

title: Allow to configure vertical scrollbar width for terminal, explorer pane, preview pane
body:
Using VSCode 1.58.2 on Ubuntu 20.04.2, Gnome 3.36.8
With version 1.58 it is possible to set the width of the scrollbar, which is great [Editor scroll bar customization](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_58#_editor-scroll-bar-customization).
However, the width of the scrollbar is still not configurable in the Explorer pane, the integrated terminal and the Markdown/Asciidoc preview. When the monitor has resolution greater than 1920x1080, it is rather difficult to grad the scrollbar. Especially with GTK3 theme where the mouse pointer is not very accurate close to the edge. Actually, it is almost impossible to grab the scrollbar. Because when the mouse is close to the edge, GTK3 theme thinks that you want to resize the window. So the mouse cursor switches to the resize window function rather than the pointy mouse cursor to allow you to grab the scrollbar.
The issue is similar on the Explorer pane with the vertical scrollbar. Although not absurdly as impossible as with the terminal scrollbar but still quite sensitive to mis-click.
Maybe this is a GTK3 design issue. Nevertheless, the issue seems to only affect VSCode. All other apps whether GTK2 or GTK3 having vertical scrollbar width comparable to that of VSCode works perfectly. I hope this could be fixed in VSCode by:
- Either review the accuracy of mouse pointer detection when close to the right edge. VSCode should not confuse grabbing the scrollbar with resizing the window width.
- OR to configure the width of the vertical scrollbar. To make it simple, one single config setting for these 3 windows enough (explorer, terminal, markdown/asciidoc preview).
![VSCode-explorer-scrollbar-width](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72353094/136496427-5126b7df-d567-4dfd-b1df-8f7dc0a350ff.png)

title: enableProposedApi is not respected
body:
Setting `"enableProposedApi": true` in `package.json` has no effect (outside of debug), but using `--enable-proposed-api` works.
- VSCode Version: ~1.22.1~ 1.23.0 insiders
- OS Version: Windows 10
1. Create brand-new typescript extension.
2. Add `"enableProposedApi": true` to `package.json` as described [here](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_21#_proposed-extension-apis)
3. Use new webview api in `extension.ts`:
```ts
let webview = vscode.window.createWebview('my-id', 'Hello', vscode.ViewColumn.Two, { });
webview.html = "Hello";
```
4. ~Hit `F5` to open up extension host.~ In insiders build `"enableProposedApi": true` in `package.json` truly allows proposed api usage for the debug session.
5. Compile and copy the extension to `%USERPROFILE%\.vscode-insiders\extensions`
6.  Call command `HelloWorld`, you'll see error in the console:
> Extension 'my.extension.id cannot use PROPOSED API (must started out of dev or enabled via --enable-proposed-api)
Running vscode with `--enable-proposed-api=my.extension.id` allows to run the extension.
- Expected:
`"enableProposedApi": true` in `package.json` should allow running the extension.
I also uploaded the sample project:
https://github.com/OrkhanAlikhanov/vscode-enable-proposed-api-bug

title: Shoudn't the shortcut for "Toggle Developer Tools" be set to "editorFocus" for "when" parameter?
body:
I was frustrated I could never open the Developer Tools with the default shortcut `Ctrl + Shift + I`.
After going through all extensions, I realized it was set to only work when Developer Tools is open: `"when": "isDevelopment"`.
Shouldn't this also be set to **"editorFocus"** and maybe even more, so its actually a toggle?  Here's how I edited my keybindings.json for others with this issue (Also added them to **"reloadWindow,"** so you didn't have to be in Developer mode):
```
{
    "key": "ctrl+shift+i",
    "command": "workbench.action.toggleDevTools",
    "when": "isDevelopment || editorFocus || terminalFocus"
},

{
    "key": "ctrl+alt+r",
    "command": "workbench.action.reloadWindow",
    "when": "isDevelopment || editorFocus || terminalFocus"
},
```

title: Hot exit with multiple windows doesn't work
body:
I don't know if this is a bug or a feature. This was first suggested in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/17548 but was closed in favour of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/15467 and then "fixed" in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/18626
But I still get prompts with `onExitAndWindowClose`. Maybe this is WAI, so I'm filing this as a feature request -- I don't want any prompts. I don't want to sift through dozens of scratch files to figure out what I need to keep or not every time I close a window. Just save them somewhere.
IntelliJ has a special slot for them in the project tree called "Scratches". Maybe add a new panel in the Explorer accordion called "Scratches" that would list them all there. Or maybe two, one for Global and one for Current Workspace. I don't really care where these scratches get saved right now, I just want rid of the prompt and know that they're *somewhere* on disk if I really need them again.
Likewise, I don't want 3 or 4 windows to open every time I run vscode because I had some scratches open when I shut down my computer. Please, can we just tuck them away somewhere?

title: Explicit keyboard binding when clause context for "Terminal in editor area"
body:
Is there an explicit keyboard binding when clause context to assign different key bindings to focused integrated & "in editor area" terminals?
If not, I'd really like to see them added. Something like `terminalEditorFocus` in addition to the already existing `terminalFocus`.
# Example use case
I have assigned `Ctrl+F4` to `workbench.action.terminal.kill` when `terminalFocus` and to `workbench.action.closeActiveEditor` as a fallback (when no terminal has focus).
However, the `terminalFocus` when clause is also true when an "in editor area terminal" has focus for which the `workbench.action.terminal.kill` doesn't work since a dedicated `workbench.action.terminal.killEditor` exists.
In other words, I'd need the following 3 key bindings:
* `Ctrl+F4` -> `workbench.action.terminal.kill` when `terminalFocus`
* `Ctrl+F4` -> `workbench.action.terminal.killEditor` when `terminalEditorFocus` (not possible due to missing when context)
* `Ctrl+F4` -> `workbench.action.closeActiveEditor` when `-`

title: Toggle auto-format on save per-language
body:
It would be nice there was a way to enable or disable auto-format on save per-language. For example:
```json
{
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.formatOnSave.disableForLanguages": ["css"]
}
```
or the opposite:
```json
{
  "editor.formatOnSave": false,
  "editor.formatOnSave.enableForLanguages": ["typescript"]
}
```
My specific use case:
- In general, I want to auto-format on save for languages such as TypeScript that have decent formatters (whose style I agree with)
- For some languages, such as SCSS and CSS, I haven't found a formatter whose style agrees with mine, so I don't want to always auto-format on save in those languages.
- BUT in those same languages, sometimes I'm looking at some code with absolutely no formatting (for example, blob of CSS styles copied from browser debugger), and any formatting style is preferable to the total lack of formatting.
In the last case, I have to enable an extension, format my code, and then re-disable the extension, which is kind of a pain.
(If someone can point me to the right place, I might be interested in implementing this. Also, apologies if this has been requested already--tried to search but may not have used the right terms.)
